questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{1}}: \quad$ If pressure of a gas is increased keeping the temperature constant the speed of sound wave in the gas increases.
$\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{2}}$ : In a travelling sinusoidal sound wave the phase difference between displacement wave and pressure wave is $\frac{\pi}{2}$.
$\mathbf{S}_3$ : $\quad$ The apparent frequency is $v_1$, when a source approaches a stationary observer with speed $u$ and is $v_2$ when the observer approaches the same stationary source with the same speed $u$. Then $v_2 \lt v_1$, if $u \lt v$ where $v$ is the speed of sound.
questions.solution:
$\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{1}}$ : When pressure is increase density also increases in same proportion
$\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{2}}$ : True
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathbf{S}_3: v_1=\frac{v}{v-u} v \\
& v_2=\left[\frac{u+v}{v}\right] v
\end{aligned}$
$v_1-v_2=v\left[\frac{v}{v-u}-\frac{u+v}{v}\right]$
$=v\left[\frac{v^2-v^2+u^2}{(v-u) v}\right]=\frac{u^2 v}{(v-u) v}\gt0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Ranvir and Akshay are rowing two boats in a river with the same speed v relative to the river. Ranvir is upstream relative to Akshay. The river is flowing with speed v/2. Each boat sends, through the water, a signal to the other. The frequencies $\mathrm{f}_0$ of the generated continuous sonic signals are the same. Each observer observes the wave sent by the other person. In each of the situation in column-I, Match the appropriate entries.
questions.solution:
Time taken by wave to travel from Ranvir to Akshay
$T_A=\frac{d}{C+\frac{v}{2}+v} ; T_B=\frac{d}{C+\frac{v}{2}-v} ; T_C=\frac{d}{C+\frac{v}{2}-v} ; T_D=\frac{d}{C+\frac{v}{2}+v}$
Time taken by wave to travel from Akshay to Ranvir
$T_A=\frac{d}{C-\frac{v}{2}-v} ; T_B=\frac{d}{C-\frac{v}{2}-v} ; T_C=\frac{d}{C-\frac{v}{2}+v} ; T_D=\frac{d}{C-\frac{v}{2}+v}$
Time will not be same for any case Wavelength observed by Akshay
$\lambda_{\mathrm{A}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}+\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2}+\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{f}} ; \lambda_{\mathrm{B}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}+\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2}+\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{f}} ; \lambda_{\mathrm{C}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}+\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2}-\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{f}} ; \lambda_{\mathrm{D}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}+\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2}-\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{f}}$
Wavelength observed by Ranvir
$\lambda_{\mathrm{A}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}-\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2}-\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{f}} ; \lambda_{\mathrm{B}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}-\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2}+\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{f}} ; \lambda_{\mathrm{C}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}-\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2}+\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{f}} ; \lambda_{\mathrm{D}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}-\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2}-\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{f}}$
Frequency observed by Akshay
$f_A=f ; f_B=f\left[\frac{C+\frac{v}{2}-v}{C+\frac{v}{2}+v}\right] ; f_C=f ; f_D=f\left[\frac{C+\frac{v}{2}+v}{C+\frac{v}{2}-v}\right]$
Frequency observed by Ranvir
$f_A=f ; f_B=f\left[\frac{C-\frac{v}{2}-v}{C-\frac{v}{2}+v}\right] ; f_C=f ; f_D=f\left[\frac{C-\frac{v}{2}+v}{C-\frac{v}{2}-v}\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The 5 graphs shown in column-II are plots of displacement from mean position at a given instant. The arrows represent the direction of velocity of particles at that moment. Match with description in column-I.
questions.solution:
- For standing wave all particles have same direction of motion.
- For longitudinal wave : Velocity of particle is along the propagation of wave.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A tube of diameter $d$ and of length / unit is open at both ends. Its fundamental frequency of resonance is found to be $v_1$. The velocity of sound in air is $330 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$. One end of tube is not closed. The lowest frequency of resonance of tube is $v_2$. Taking into consideration the end correction, $\frac{v_2}{v_1}$ is
questions.solution:
for open pipe $f_l=V_1=\frac{V}{2(l+2 e)}$ for closed pipe $f_c=V_2=\frac{V}{4(l+e)}$
But $e=0.3 d$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Three pieces of string each of length $L$ are joined end to end to make a string of length 3L, the linear mass densities of strings are $\mu, 4 \mu$ and $9 \mu$ respectively. One end of the combined string is tied to fixed support and the string is stretched to a tension T. A transverse wave has been generated at the other end having frequency F.
Question: Find the ratio of wavelengths of waves on three strings
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} A_{2+} & =A \frac{2 V_2}{V_2+V_3} \\ & =A \frac{2 \frac{1}{2} V_1}{\frac{1}{2} V_1+\frac{1}{3} V_1}=\frac{6}{5} \mathrm{~A} \\ A_{1+} & =\frac{6 \mathrm{~A}}{5} \times \frac{2 V_1}{V_1+V_2}=\frac{6 \mathrm{~A}}{5} \times \frac{2 V_1}{V_1+\frac{V_1}{2}} \\ & =\frac{8 \mathrm{~A}}{5}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

For $\mathrm{k}=100 \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$, phase difference between two particles at $\mathrm{x}_1=2 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $x_2=4 \mathrm{~cm}$ at $t=0$ is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A string of length $l$ is stretched by $\frac{l}{10}$ and the velocity of transverse waves along it is V . When it is stretched by $\frac{l}{20}$ (assuming Hooke's law is applicable), the velocity of waves will become:
questions.solution:
$T \alpha \Delta l \quad V=\sqrt{\frac{T}{\mu}} \quad=\sqrt{\frac{k \Delta l}{\frac{m}{l}}}=\sqrt{\frac{k(\Delta l) l}{m}}$
$\therefore V \alpha \sqrt{(\Delta l) l} \quad \therefore \frac{V_2}{V_1}=\sqrt{\frac{\left(\Delta l_2\right) \times l_2}{\left(\Delta l_1\right) \times l_1}}=\sqrt{\frac{(0.05) \times 1.05}{0.1 \times 1.10}}$
$\therefore \frac{V_2}{V_1}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \quad \therefore V_2=\frac{V}{\sqrt{2}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
